channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Futility of AI-Powered Weapons Scanners in NYC Subway Stations: A Pilot Program's Alarming Results
item.description: New York City's pilot program testing AI-powered weapons scanners in subway stations results in over 100 false positives and 12 knives detected, raising concerns about the effectiveness and constitutionality of such measures.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russia amplified hurricane disinformation to drive Americans apart, researchers find
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Russia has helped amplify and spread false and misleading internet claims about recent hurricanes in the United States and the federal government's response, part of a wider effort by the Kremlin to manipulate America's political discourse before the presidential election, new rese
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: White House Unveils Framework to Balance AI Benefits and Risks
item.description: The White House has announced a new framework to govern the use of artificial intelligence by U.S. national security and spy agencies, aiming to balance the technology's potential with its risks.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Two new HHS programs to help with complicated health challenges
item.description: "A lot of the efforts that we're doing across the government have a plug into the artificial intelligence world," said Andy Kilianski. The post Two new HHS programs to help with complicated health challenges first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to prepare your online accounts for when you die
item.description: Most people have accumulated a pile of data on their social media and digital accounts over their lifetimes.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: As coal plants shut in Romania, some miners transition to green energy while others are reluctant
item.description: PETRILA, Romania (AP) — For many years, Sebastian Tirinticǎ worked in a coal mine, just like his father and grandfather before him.
item.dc:creator: ANCA GURZU, Cipher News, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Meet Capacities, the do-it-all productivity app taking on Notion
item.description: Here’s a tantalizing little paradox to chew over: One of the most interesting up-and-coming productivity apps is also one of the most immediately familiar-feeling—on the surface, at least. The app is called Capacities.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: Jet-tracker who watched Zuckerberg, Trump, Musk says Meta has banned his accounts
item.description: Meta has removed accounts that track the plane movements of its CEO Mark Zuckerberg, X owner Elon Musk, Jeff Bezos, Kim Kardashian, former President Trump and others, account owner Jack Sweeney said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: What a Trump win could mean for Elon Musk's businesses
item.description: Elon Musk is wearing multiple hats this election season, sparking questions about how his dual role as a tech leader and vocal surrogate for former President Trump could lead to conflicts of interest.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Big offshore wind project proposed for New York as other sites are evaluated in 3 states
item.description: BRIGANTINE, N.J. (AP) — Offshore wind energy projects in New York, New Jersey and Maryland are moving forward, as federal regulators examine the proposals and opponents escalate their legal challenges to the work.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Asana launches AI Studio, a no-code tool for building AI agents
item.description: Asana has launched AI Studio, a no-code tool for building generative AI agents that can automate work planning tasks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Intel, AMD forge x86 alliance
item.description: The old adage “the enemy of my enemy is my ally” has been borne out again, with AMD and Intel joining forces with a number of their largest partners to create the x86 Ecosystem Advisory Group, a consortium that aims to drive forward the ecosystem around x86 processors.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Don’t panic. Here’s how to find your photos after iOS 18 update
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson has the details on the iOS 18 Photos app: Revamped layout, customizable collections and easier photo organization.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why Python is the language of choice for AI
item.description: The widespread adoption of AI is creating a paradigm shift in the software engineering world. Python has quickly become the programming language of choice for AI development due to its usability, mature ecosystem, and ability to meet the data-driven needs of AI and machine learning (ML) workflows.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: One of the world’s largest book publishers adds AI warnings to its books
item.description: To make generative AI tools (genAI) work as well as possible, tech companies have chosen to train their large language models (LLMs) on large amounts of text, even though doing so could run afoul of copyright laws.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Biden administration to provide $325 million for new Michigan semiconductor factory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Slack researcher discusses the fear, loathing and excitement surrounding AI in the workplace
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Artificial intelligence's recent rise to the forefront of business has left most office workers wondering how often they should use the technology and whether a computer will eventually replace them.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI needs Apple, which is why time is on its side
item.description: Apple was behind on smartphones, until it wasn’t. It lagged the crowd in digital music players, until it didn’t. There was a time when it was woeful on wearables, until it that changed.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: IBM works to address the developer skills gap with AI
item.description: When IBM announced in May 2023 that its generative artificial intelligence tools were in use by its AI assistant, watsonx, to help enterprises modernize their mainframe applications by translating COBOL code into Java, it was just the beginning of its efforts to leverage generative AI for developers
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here's how to stay safe from scams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, which means it's the perfect time to learn how to protect yourself from scams. “Scams have become so sophisticated now.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA MORGA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US to probe Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' system after pedestrian killed in low visibility conditions
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — The U.S. government's road safety agency is investigating Tesla's “Full Self-Driving” system after getting reports of crashes in low-visibility conditions, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, Fox 5
